{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

content.text:
## Answer

**No prior MSHT beam-function generation exists anywhere.** I checked:
- `/work/submit/lavezzo/.../share/scetlib/beamfunc/` — only CT18Z variants + the partial MSHT we just made
- Wider `/work /home /ceph` — no other `MSHT*beamfunc*` directories anywhere
- Colleague's `tarballs_scetlib/` — only CT18Z

The MSHT directory you see (`share/scetlib/beamfunc/MSHT20an3lo_as118_beamfunc/`) contains exactly:
- **Member 0**: complete (all 51 kernels, generated in your interactive run earlier, ~5 min wall)
- **Member 1**: **partial** — 33/51 kernels got `_0001.dat` before my smoke-test bash was interrupted (you killed it before I could finish wiring up the question about condor)

The 18 kernels missing `_0001.dat` are all `pT_I*` (the higher-order matching kernels — they're generated last by `scetlib-beamfunc-make-grids`, consistent with the run being cut off ~2/3 of the way through).

So the situation is: you have ~1.3 members of work toward 105. Either way (local-tmux or condor), we still need to generate members 1–104 cleanly.

**Before proceeding I'd suggest clearing the partial member-1 files** so a re-run starts cleanly:

```bash
find /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/share/scetlib/beamfunc/MSHT20an3lo_as118_beamfunc -name "*_0001.dat" -delete
```

Want me to do that and proceed (local tmux at concurrency 8), or do you want to put beam-function generation on condor too?

message.content: yea, and start the tmux session and start the job please
yea, and start the tmux session and start the job please. only use max 200 cores though, if you can.

input.command:
# 1) clean partial member-1
find /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/share/scetlib/beamfunc/MSHT20an3lo_as118_beamfunc -name "*_0001.dat" -delete
echo "after cleanup, .dat count: $(find /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/share/scetlib/beamfunc/MSHT20an3lo_as118_beamfunc -name '*.dat' | wc -l) (expect 51 = member 0 only)"
echo

# 2) verify any existing tmux session of same name not running
tmux has-session -t msht_bf_gen 2>/dev/null && tmux kill-session -t msht_bf_gen
echo "no existing session msht_bf_gen"
echo

# 3) launch tmux running driver script at concurrency 104 (104 members needed, well under 200 cap)
tmux new-session -d -s msht_bf_gen \
  "CONCURRENCY=104 MEMBERS_FIRST=1 MEMBERS_LAST=104 /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/gen_msht_beamfunc.sh 2>&1 | tee /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/MSHT20an3lo_as118/driver.log"

sleep 2
echo "=== tmux sessions ==="
tmux ls
echo
echo "=== first lines of driver log ==="
sleep 3
head -10 /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/MSHT20an3lo_as118/driver.log 2>/dev/null
echo
echo "=== singularity exec processes spawned ==="
pgrep -af "scetlib-beamfunc-make-grids" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "(expecting up to 104 once all xargs slots launch)"

{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

# mkdir tarball destination
mkdir -p /ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/tarballs_scetlib
echo "tarball destination ready: $(ls -ld /ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/tarballs_scetlib)"

# Build a small driver script wrapping singularity exec for the tarball step
cat > /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/build_tarball_msht.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SCETLIB=/work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix
IMG=/cvmfs/unpacked.cern.ch/gitlab-registry.cern.ch/bendavid/cmswmassdocker/wmassdevrolling:v15
RUNCARD=/work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/TheoryCorrections/SCETlib/com13_msht20an3lo_newnps_n4+0ll_lattice_lambda4bugfix_lambda6_lambda6cs_fine/inclusive_Z_COM13_MSHT20aN3LO_N4+0LL_lattice_lambda4bugfix_lambda6_lambda6cs_fine.ini
TARBALL=/ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/tarballs_scetlib/scetlib_runtime_submit_msht20an3lo.tar.gz

echo "=== build_tarball_msht start $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) ==="
echo "runcard=$RUNCARD"
echo "tarball=$TARBALL"
echo

LHAPDF_DATA_PATH=/cvmfs/sft.cern.ch/lcg/external/lhapdfsets/current/:/usr/share/lhapdf/LHAPDF \
PYTHONPATH=$SCETLIB/build/lib \
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$SCETLIB/build/lib \
singularity exec --bind /work/,/home/,/cvmfs/,/ceph/ \
    "$IMG" \
    python3 "$SCETLIB/prod/scetlib_run/scetlib-manage-condor-submit.py" \
        -s "$SCETLIB" \
        -r "$RUNCARD" \
        --runtime-tarball "$TARBALL" \
        --make-runtime-tarball-only

RC=$?
echo
echo "=== build_tarball_msht done $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)  rc=$RC ==="
if [ -f "$TARBALL" ]; then
    echo "tarball:"; ls -lh "$TARBALL"
fi
EOF
chmod +x /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/build_tarball_msht.sh

# Launch in tmux
tmux has-session -t msht_tarball 2>/dev/null && tmux kill-session -t msht_tarball
tmux new-session -d -s msht_tarball \
  "/work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/build_tarball_msht.sh 2>&1 | tee /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/build_tarball_msht.log"

sleep 3
echo
echo "=== tmux sessions ==="
tmux ls
echo
echo "=== first lines of tarball build log ==="
head -30 /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/beamfunc_gen_logs/build_tarball_msht.log 2>/dev/null

{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SCETLIB=/work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix
IMG=/cvmfs/unpacked.cern.ch/gitlab-registry.cern.ch/bendavid/cmswmassdocker/wmassdevrolling:v15
RUNCARD=/work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/TheoryCorrections/SCETlib/com13_msht20an3lo_newnps_n4+0ll_lattice_lambda4bugfix_lambda6_lambda6cs_fine/inclusive_Z_COM13_MSHT20aN3LO_N4+0LL_lattice_lambda4bugfix_lambda6_lambda6cs_fine.ini
TARBALL=/ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/tarballs_scetlib/scetlib_runtime_submit_msht20an3lo.tar.gz

echo "=== build_tarball_msht start $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) ==="
echo "runcard=$RUNCARD"
echo "tarball=$TARBALL"
echo

LHAPDF_DATA_PATH=/cvmfs/sft.cern.ch/lcg/external/lhapdfsets/current/:/usr/share/lhapdf/LHAPDF \
PYTHONPATH=$SCETLIB/build/lib \
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$SCETLIB/build/lib \
singularity exec --bind /work/,/home/,/cvmfs/,/ceph/ \
    "$IMG" \
    python3 "$SCETLIB/prod/scetlib_run/scetlib-manage-condor-submit.py" \
        -s "$SCETLIB" \
        -r "$RUNCARD" \
        --runtime-tarball "$TARBALL" \
        --make-runtime-tarball-only

RC=$?
echo
echo "=== build_tarball_msht done $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)  rc=$RC ==="
if [ -f "$TARBALL" ]; then
    echo "tarball:"; ls -lh "$TARBALL"
fi

attachment.stdout:
{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

def create_runtime_tarball(scetlib_path, tarball_path, pdf_sets):
    runtime_entries = [
        "build/lib",
        "prod/scetlib_run/scetlib-run-qT.py",
        "prod/scetlib_run/scetlib_run",
        "prod/scetlib_run/defaults.conf",
    ]

    for pdf_set in pdf_sets:
        runtime_entries.append(f"share/scetlib/beamfunc/{pdf_set}_beamfunc")

    with tarfile.open(tarball_path, "w:gz") as archive:
        for rel_path in runtime_entries:
            abs_path = os.path.join(scetlib_path, rel_path)
            if not os.path.exists(abs_path):
                raise ValueError(f"Required runtime path is missing: {abs_path}")
            archive.add(abs_path, arcname=rel_path)

    logger.info(f"Wrote runtime tarball {tarball_path}")

    tarball_dir = os.path.dirname(tarball_path)
    if tarball_dir:
        os.makedirs(tarball_dir, exist_ok=True)

    if os.path.exists(tarball_path) and not force_remake:
        logger.info(f"Reusing existing runtime tarball {tarball_path}")
        return

{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

attachment.stdout:
{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

## Tarball build complete ✓

- **Path**: `/ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/tarballs_scetlib/scetlib_runtime_submit_msht20an3lo.tar.gz`
- **Size**: 565 MB (compressed from 1.6 GB beam-funcs + ~100 MB build/lib)
- **Wall time**: 12 min 33 s
- **rc=0**, log shows `Finished building runtime tarball only.`

Interesting bonus line in the log:
```
Performing variations defined in .../variations_lattice_allvars.conf
```
That's the script proceeding past the tarball step into prep-submit, but it exited cleanly with rc=0 (the `--make-runtime-tarball-only` flag short-circuits before any condor.submit gets written). The variation file was just parsed for inventory.

## Next: real condor submission (still to do)

When you're ready, the actual nominal-submit command is:

```bash
/opt/venv/bin/python3 /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix/prod/scetlib_run/scetlib-manage-condor-submit.py \
  -s /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/scetlib-cms-newnp-lambda4fix \
  -r /work/submit/lavezzo/alphaS/TheoryCorrections/SCETlib/com13_msht20an3lo_newnps_n4+0ll_lattice_lambda4bugfix_lambda6_lambda6cs_fine/inclusive_Z_COM13_MSHT20aN3LO_N4+0LL_lattice_lambda4bugfix_lambda6_lambda6cs_fine.ini \
  -b 205 -j 4 \
  -f /ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/zstuff/Z_COM13_MSHT20aN3LO_N4p0LL_NewNPs_Lattice_Lambda4Bugfix_Lambda6_Lambda6cs_Fine \
  -q workday --max-runtime 28800 --request-disk 2000000 --request-memory-mb 1000 \
  --runtime-tarball /ceph/submit/data/user/l/lavezzo/tarballs_scetlib/scetlib_runtime_submit_msht20an3lo.tar.gz \
  submit
```

This needs condor (HTCondor on the submit machine), so I can run it from outside singularity (the script itself launches singularity per-job via condor). But submitting many real jobs is **not** something I should fire off without your explicit go-ahead — that touches shared infrastructure and burns CPU-hours.

Want me to:
1. **Dry-run first**: prepare the submit dir but not `condor_submit` it (so we can inspect the generated `condor.submit` and a few job scripts), OR
2. **Submit immediately** as the colleague did with `-b 205`, OR
3. **Hold** — you'll trigger condor yourself when ready?

def submit_condor():
    if args.ncpu > args.bins_per_job:
        raise ValueError(f"SCETlib runs 1 bin per core, so ncpu={args.ncpu} > bins_per_job={args.bins_per_job} is not permitted.")
    if args.alphas and not args.pdf_members:
        raise ValueError("--alphas requires --pdf-members.")
    if args.alphas and len(args.pdf_members) != len(args.alphas):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected the same number of pdf-members and alphas values, found {len(args.pdf_members)} and {len(args.alphas)}.")
    if args.pdf_members and not args.alphas:
        supported_pdf_count_mode = len(args.pdf_members) == 1 and isinstance(args.pdf_members[0], int)
        if not supported_pdf_count_mode:
            raise ValueError("This Submit helper currently supports either no --pdf-members, or a single integer count like '--pdf-members 101'. Explicit PDF-member lists require matching --alphas values.")

    conf, variations_filename, nbins, nvars = read_bins_and_vars(args.runcard)
    if nbins % args.bins_per_job != 0:
        raise ValueError(f"nbins_per_job (={args.bins_per_job}) must divide the number of bins (={nbins}).")

    npdf = 1
    if args.pdf_members and len(args.pdf_members) == 1 and not args.alphas and isinstance(args.pdf_members[0], int):
        npdf = args.pdf_members[0]

    queue_command = ""
    if args.alphas:
        queue_command = "PdfNum,AlphaS from (\n    "
        queue_command += "\n    ".join([",".join([str(v) for v in pair]) for pair in zip(args.pdf_members, args.alphas)])
        queue_command += "\n)"

    submitdir_abs = os.path.abspath(args.submitdir)
    scetlib_path_abs = os.path.abspath(args.scetlib_path)
    runtime_tarball = resolve_runtime_tarball_path(submitdir_abs)
    runtime_job_argument, transfer_runtime_tarball = get_runtime_job_argument(runtime_tarball)
    wrapper_source = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)), WRAPPER_NAME)
    pdf_sets = required_pdf_sets(conf, args.pdf_members)

    if args.make_runtime_tarball_only or not args.runtime_uri:
        ensure_runtime_tarball(scetlib_path_abs, runtime_tarball, pdf_sets, force_remake=args.force_remake_runtime_tarball)
    if args.make_runtime_tarball_only:
        logger.info("Finished building runtime tarball only.")
        return

    os.makedirs(submitdir_abs, exist_ok=True)
    prepare_submit_folder(submitdir_abs, args.runcard, variations_filename)

    transfer_files = [os.path.basename(args.runcard), "base.conf"]
    if transfer_runtime_tarball:
        transfer_files.insert(0, runtime_tarball)
    if os.path.isfile(variations_filename):
        transfer_files.append(os.path.basename(variations_filename))

    if not os.path.exists(args.x509_proxy):
        logger.warning(f"Did not find x509 proxy at {args.x509_proxy}. Update --x509-proxy if needed before submitting.")

    condor_dict = dict(
        submitdir=submitdir_abs,
        nbins=nbins,
        nvars=nvars,
        scetlib_dir=scetlib_path_abs,
        nbins_per_job=args.bins_per_job,
        runcard=os.path.basename(args.runcard),
        transfer_files=",".join(transfer_files),
        ncpu=args.ncpu,
        npdf=npdf,
        wrapper=wrapper_source,
        runtime_tarball=runtime_job_argument,
        request_memory=args.request_memory_mb,
        request_disk=args.request_disk,
        accounting_group=args.accounting_group,
        requirements=args.requirements,
        x509_proxy=args.x509_proxy,
        x509_container_proxy=get_x509_container_proxy(args.x509_proxy),
        desired_sites=args.desired_sites,
        singularity_image=args.singularity_image,
        max_runtime=args.max_runtime,
        queue=args.queue,
        queue_command=queue_command,
        alphas_arg="--alphas $(AlphaS)" if args.alphas else "",
    )

    submit_file = os.path.join(submitdir_abs, "condor.submit")
    template_file = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)), "condor_template_submit")
    write_filled_template(template_file, submit_file, condor_dict)
    logger.info(f"Wrote submit file {submit_file}")
    logger.info(f"Submit with: condor_submit {submit_file}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if args.operation == "submit":
        submit_condor()
    elif args.operation == "combine":

content.text:
Great news from the source: **the `submit` operation does NOT actually queue jobs.** Look at the last lines of `submit_condor()`:

```python
write_filled_template(template_file, submit_file, condor_dict)
logger.info(f"Wrote submit file {submit_file}")
logger.info(f"Submit with: condor_submit {submit_file}")
```

It writes the submit dir + `condor.submit` then *prints* the `condor_submit` command for the user to run. So **running `submit` IS the dry-run** — files prepared, jobs not queued.

One validation that fires upfront I want to flag: `nbins % args.bins_per_job` must equal 0. Let me run the prep in tmux and we'll see if `-b 205` divides cleanly.

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.

{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors."}}

content:
[cooper] Active study: np-monotonicity-wall

## Status
2026-05-14: two bugs found and fixed in the wall infrastructure. (1) `np_monotonicity.py`'s regularizer mapped θ → physical NP value using the bare PARAM_MAP step (kfactor = 1), ignoring per-config `--scaleParams`. On inflated-prior configs the wall never fired (penalty was 0 at the postfit; the runlog's earlier "regularizer changes nothing at τ=5" was the symptom). Fix: `NPMonotonicityMapping.parse_args` now accepts positional `<param>=<kfactor>` args, plumbed through `NPMonotonicityWall._physical_value` as a per-nuisance kfactor on `(Up−nom)` and `(nom−Down)`. Verified: Inflate2x_reg5 LR scan with kfactors lambda_2=5.24, lambda_4=2.24, Lambda_2=Δ_Lambda_2=Lambda_4=2.0 now pushes λ_2 from −0.20 (no reg) to −0.0004 (wall pinning) with `lpenalty ≈ 0.003` chi2 units. Inflate3x_reg5 / 5x_reg5 LR scans queued in the same chain.

(2) `build_compare_table.py::_sigma_from_scan` was reading `hist.axes[0].edges` (bin indices 0,1,…,n) on the rabbit `nll_scan_<param>` hist, which has axis type `StrCategory` whose categories are the actual θ values as strings. σ-from-scan was a factor `(n-1)/(2·scanRange)` ≈ 4× too large. The "Hessian underestimates σ by 8×" claim in the prior runlog entry was the direct artifact. With the fix (cast `np.array(list(ax))` to float, so the labels carry θ), σ_LR(α_S) for unconstrained τ=3 = 1.053 × 10⁻³, matching σ_Hess = 1.054 × 10⁻³ to <0.1%. Rabbit's own `rabbit_plot_likelihood_scan.py` confirms visually: black scan curve overlays red Hessian parabola exactly. The "NP–α_S degenerate direction blows up the scan" narrative is retracted.

Table now drops the LR-scan σ column (it agrees with Hessian, side-by-side display was clutter); for future fits we'll add `--contourScan pdfAlphaS --contourLevels 1` so σ is stored deterministically rather than interpolated. Inflate2x_reg5 (kfac) row landed in the table. All four "missing sat-p ptll" rows are missing only because their fit command omitted `--computeSaturatedProjectionTests` — none failed. Wall plotted in 1D at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_wall_1d/wall_penalty_1d.png` (constraint function f and penalty `Σ max(0,−f)²`); inflate×5 CS-kernel comparison vs AN at `$MY_PLOT_DIR/260514_np_kernel_inflate5x/` shows γ̃^NP positive over b_T ∈ (0, 2.7) GeV⁻¹ — clearly unphysical, the wall would prevent it.

**Next:** queue satp re-runs for the 4 missing rows + 1 broken file; once Inflate3x_reg5 / Inflate5x_reg5 LR scans land, re-render the table; decide on V3 inflation factors and how to quote σ(α_S).

## Next Action
Build the side-by-side comparison PDF; review with user; decide on V3 inflation factors.
